---
title: "Quarto Tutorial: Basic Chart.js"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
execute:
    echo: true
---

# What this is

Quick primer on working with a `.qmd` file: YAML up top, Markdown prose, and executable code cells. Render with `quarto preview testing.qmd` or `quarto render testing.qmd` to produce HTML in the same folder.


# Anatomy of a .qmd

````markdown
---
title: "My Doc"
format: html
---

Some prose.

```{python}
print("hello from python")
```
````

# Running a small code cell

Below is a simple Python chunk. It will run when you render.

```{python}
nums = list(range(5))
total = sum(nums)
nums, total
```

# Embedding Chart.js (no build tools)

HTML in a chunk is passed through as-is, so you can include a `<canvas>` and a script that pulls Chart.js from a CDN. On render, the chart will appear in the output page.

```{html}
<canvas id="myChart" height="120"></canvas>
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.4.1/dist/chart.umd.min.js"></script>
<script>
	const ctx = document.getElementById('myChart');
	const chart = new Chart(ctx, {
		type: 'bar',
		data: {
			labels: ['A', 'B', 'C', 'D'],
			datasets: [{
				label: 'Demo Values',
				data: [3, 7, 4, 6],
				backgroundColor: ['#4c6ef5', '#15aabf', '#e64980', '#fab005']
			}]
		},
		options: {
			responsive: true,
			plugins: {
				legend: { position: 'bottom' },
				title: { display: true, text: 'Chart.js inside Quarto' }
			},
			scales: {
				y: { beginAtZero: true }
			}
		}
	});
</script>
```

# Notes

- Use `echo: true/false` in YAML or per-chunk to toggle code visibility.
- Add `warning: false` or `message: false` in chunk options to silence outputs when needed.
- Switching formats is just `format: pdf` or `format: revealjs` in the YAML when you have the right tooling installed.
